Test Case Description: Verify successful login with valid standard_user credentials
Pre-Condition: User is on SauceDemo login page (https://www.saucedemo.com/)
Test Steps: 1. Open https://www.saucedemo.com/ ; 2. Enter "standard_user" in username field ; 3. Enter "secret_sauce" in password field ; 4. Click Login button
Expected Result: User is redirected to Swag Labs inventory page (URL contains "/inventory.html") and sees product catalog
Misc (Comments): Inference (low confidence): PRD not provided – derived from site behavior and public documentation

Actual Result: though login may take >2 seconds longer than standard_user
Priority: Inference (low confidence): based on username hint "performance_glitch"

Test Case Description: Verify login fails with incorrect password

Expected Result: User stays on login page and sees error: "Epic sadface: Username and password do not match any user in this service"
Misc (Comments): Expected error derived from standard login failure behavior (publicly known)

Expected Result: User remains on login page with error: "Epic sadface: Username is required"

Test Case Description: Verify user can log out successfully from inventory page
Pre-Condition: User is logged in and on inventory page
Test Steps: 1. Click on hamburger menu (top-left) ; 2. Click "Logout" link ; 3. Verify return to login page
Expected Result: User is redirected to https://www.saucedemo.com/ and login form is visible. Cart/state is cleared per session
Misc (Comments): Inference (medium confidence) from standard web app session behavior

Test Case Description: Measure login response time for performance_glitch_user vs standard_user
Pre-Condition: User on login page with browser DevTools or performance tool
Test Steps: 1. Record start time; 2. Enter credentials; 3. Click Login; 4. Record time until inventory page fully loads; 5. Compare with standard_user (TC-001)
Expected Result: performance_glitch_user login should be slower (>=2 seconds additional delay) but still complete successfully
Misc (Comments): Inference (low confidence): based on username literal description; no PRD provided

Scenario: Non-Functional: Security - No session reuse after logout
Pre-Condition: previous session token cannot access protected pages
Test Steps: User logged in and then logged out
Expected Result: 1. Log in as standard_user; 2. Copy session cookie/token; 3. Log out; 4. Attempt to access /inventory.html directly with copied token
Actual Result: Request is rejected and user is redirected to login page
